review: 1/10 - Absolute Trash, AI Slop from Activision's Copy-Paste Factory What a steaming pile of recycled garbage. Black Ops 7 isn't a game it's a lazy Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V job from the same overpaid devs who've been phoning it in since Black Ops 2. Every single map? Identical reskins of Nuketown, Rust, and Shipment with a fresh coat of futuristic gray paint that looks like it was generated by Midjourney on the fritz. Campaign? Oh please it's the exact same hero soldier vs. evil Russian/Chinese/made-up-terrorist plot we've endured for 15 years, but now with dialogue so wooden and predictable it screams ChatGPT wrote this script while hungover. And the gameplay? Holy hell, it FEELS like it was made by AI. Enemies teleporting through walls like glitchy NPCs from a Unity asset flip. Gunplay so floaty and unresponsive, it's like the recoil patterns were randomized by a neural net that hates fun. Multiplayer matches end in 30 seconds because the skill-based matchmaking is broken beyond repair pairing noobs with sweats every time, or infinite lobbies of cheaters with aimbot headsnaps smoother than a deepfake porn vid. Zombies mode? A buggy mess where waves spawn inside you, and perks glitch out mid-round because the procedurally generated hordes are just copy-pasted from BO6 with zero tweaks. Activision, fire your entire team and let actual humans make games again. This is peak soulless corporate slop 0 innovation, 100% AI hallucinations masquerading as next-gen. Refunded in 2 hours. Never touching another CoD again.

review: I was playing Battlefield 6, and after a few beers, I sort of craved a bit of a more arcade feeling for a while. I had been very hesitant on buying this game for a long time, after having religiously bought all the new generation Call of Duties. But I was like, hey, it's late, there's nothing else to do, maybe you'll have fun. Wrong. This may be the worst Call of Duty ever created in the modern era. How can a game be so bad? Well, allow me to explain what I think it is that makes this game a bland abomination. - Aesthetics and identity: There is barely any. Everything has been reduced to the same kitsch-looking operators and the same goofy weapons. In their effort to cater to sweats, they made every map look like something out of a mobile game. Flat surfaces, flat lighting, barely any detail. And lets not even get into the AI-generated Studio Ghibli-style calling cards that look like they were scraped off a prompt farm at 3am. - Gameplay: Gunplay may be sort of okay, but the movement feels like it was designed by someone who watched clips of good players and panicked. Its twitchy without being responsive, fast without any sense of weight. Everyone is either sliding around like theyre on ice or abusing whatever the current broken mechanic is. Theres no rhythm to fights anymore, just erratic bursts of nonsense where whoever breaks the animation system harder wins. - Cringe factor: Oh god, this is worse than Forza Horizon 5. So bad. The announcers sound like theyre trying to hype up a sponsored esports event for 14-year-olds. Constant forced energy, fake intensity, zero atmosphere. Operators dont talk like soldiers, they talk like TikTok personalities reading pre-approved badass lines. Its exhausting to listen to for more than two matches. - Music: The whole soundtrack sounds like something I could generate in a couple nights using Suno AI. Generic epic noise with no memorable themes, no identity, nothing that sticks. You could swap it with royalty-free YouTube music and not notice. - Maps: None of them are memorable. Its like fighting inside a series of slightly different grey rectangles. This doesnt feel like a game made by people who enjoy shooters. It feels like a product assembled by committee, tuned by metrics, and shipped the moment it hit acceptable engagement levels. It's almost like this game was generated rather than designed.
categories: Usability Issues, New Mechanic Reception, Competitive Analysis, Monetization & Value

review: Time to completely revise this review, because oh boy... With season 4, CoD has introduced new anti-cheat enforcement measures to combat their cheating problem, which includes checking for Secure Boot and TPM 2.0. Because this is Big Brother Microslop we're talking about, its being done using their fancy new attestation software, Microsoft Azure. Basically if you fail the attestation check, you're almost completely locked out of the game; You can't play Endgame at all. You only get 1 playlist each in MP and Warzone (Nuketown 24/7 and BR Casual, respectively) and you can only play zombies solo. If you do decide to play like this, you are locked out of crossplay AND isolated into a completely separate matchmaking pool AND unable to que with any of your friends if they passed the Attestation requirements. BO7 was completely fine from launch until now, but because I and many others just so happen to be one of the unlucky few with bugged hardware, now I can't play the $70 game I paid for. To make matters worse, this problem is beyond an OS problem; its a BIOS issue. The attestation is bugged for certain motherboards with certain BIOS/Secure Boot versions. In my case my motherboard manufacturer is HP, who is dragging their feet to send out updates. The only way to fix this problem if you are one of the affected few is to either A). Put down for a new motherboard entirely, B). Pray that one of the many shot-in-the-dark solutions exist and magically work for you or C). Rebuy the game on console. Shoutout to the PC industry for knowing that this bug has existed for months before launch, even prior to the launch of BO7's Open Beta. Extra Shoutout to HP, who also knew about this problem, and has done ABSOLUTELY NOTHING to fix it for anyone who doesn't own one of a few specific OMEN model gaming laptops. The actual game is ok, the MP is just fine, Warzone is Warzone, and honestly the highlight for the game is zombies, which is good. But I can't in good faith justify this purchase, and moreover I highly DO NOT recommend buying this game here on steam, to anyone for any reason. There are better PC games who respect your time and money and don't cut you out just because Activision can't handle making a proper anti-cheat, and that doesn't just include the CoD franchise itself. If, for any reason, your masochistic mind wants to play this game regardless of its AI slop campaign, romp stomp simulator MP, weakest warzone expierence, or its zombies mode that LAGS IN SOLO PLAY, please do yourself a favor and triple check that you aren't one of the unlucky few with bugged PCs or buy it on console.

review: From a long term Call of Duty player, this is one of the worst video games I have ever played. Nothing about this game feels smooth or flows well. Super unoptimized and huge file sizes as well as AI generated art everywhere with the gall to ask for $70. I bought this game on sale and still feel scammed. Weapon balance? What's that? Every weapon has basically an instant time to kill. I really hate it. If you don't have attachments, just don't even bother to use create a class. Pick up somebody else's weapon that has attachments. Every single one of my lobbies is so sweaty. Every match I get into is half over and scorestreaks are dominating the lobby already. I don't hate scorestreaks, but this game's matchmaking is awful. The game is extremely anti-PC players, with aim assist being absolutely ridiculous (as a former console player). Graphics are prioritized over common sense and basic game design. Screen shake is ridiculous. Grenade spam is still a huge problem. Many perks are still too powerful. At some level, even in public matches, everyone uses the same few guns and perks. You often spawn right next to people as well. Map design has improved since MW2019, but that shouldn't be a feat. This game is an insult to Black Ops 2's legacy. Forget visual clarity, forget weapon balance, and forget trying to make the game fun for actual good players. I don't understand why you're rewarded so much for playing so slowly in this game. You don't get any reward for playing aggressive. I am a former high level call of duty zombies player as well, but I cannot, in good conscious even acknowledge the quality of the zombies because of the disregard for the Zombies community since Black Ops Cold War. Bosses are still giant bullet sponges. The difficulty of the game has been completed destroyed aside from cursed mode, which doesn't even provide hardcore players with what they want to play off-rip: instead we MUST endure the really frustrating experience of Modern Zombies. For that reason alone, I don't even want to play zombies. I've speedran zombies and have completed almost every Zombies easter egg.

review: Been playing black ops 2 and mw2 2009 for a number of years now. Only recently I got a huge upgrade in PC. I decided to check out bo7, especially after seeing raid and hijacked and nuketown maps. Only reason I bought the game was for this. I have to say, as a father and husband, this is the perfect game. Want to quickly blow off a little steam? Hop on a quick match. Want to stop thinking for a while and unwind? Play a round or two. Its very satisfying to eliminate people ( cannot stand how we cant just call it kill) and the animations for the weapons and movement are smooth. Black ops 2 and mw2 were my go to games for a while. So if Im in the mood for a more polished and streamlined call of duty, I may just hop on this game! Overall the multiplayer is essentially the same premise as its been since multiplayer was a thing in the franchise. Only I believe they have perfected movement, movement speed, and the fluidity of weapon animations. As for the campaign I have nothing good to say about it. Its a joke and a waste of time. I would say get the game if you want to see how black ops 2 maps look in late 2025s graphics. If you find it on sale I say buy it. Dont be like me and pay $70. Worst decision of the month. Because all youre really paying for is that multiplayer experience. Have not touched zombies. Im not big on cod zombies if I want to play that Id play left 4 dead.

review: Its the worst CoD... Activision knows the formula for producing FOMO and making the game easier and easier for new players, with aim assist so strong that sometimes its very hard to tell whether someone is cheating or not., its almost impossible to perform at the same level on mouse and keyboard with all the overcomplicated movement mechanics and aim assist. They have a patented SBMM system that gives advantages based on different factors just to create the feeling that youre actually good (for example: after losing five matches in a row), and the weapons lack personality, meaning, and fun. Im part of the problem too, because I had to buy this trash just to level up a few weapons so I could play with friends, and thats exactly the problem, and why theyre sitting in such an arrogant comfort zone... The worst part is that theyre leaving aside the most important thing: that it should be fun and a game we actually enjoy. Thats why pro players hate MW19 but the general public loves it, and it wasnt because of COVID, no matter what people say. It was the mechanics, it was a simplified map, it was a balance point between controller and mouse and keyboard. It would be so easy to fix, but they keep choosing the same money-squeezing formula, thinking the franchise and the brand are so strong that theyre untouchable... Activision is the cancer that started this model, and despite the criticism and the reviews, it makes so much money that investors are happy, even if they dont care about the state of the game, the reviews, or anything else... The ultimate expression of the cancer in this industry.

review: Black Ops 7... where to begin? Zombies is fun. You can play Survival mode, where you try to survive as many round as possible. You can play Standard story mode where you kill zombies, solve puzzles, and find the easter eggs that progress the story. You can play Directed story mode, where it gives you hints on how to progress through the story. These 3 are the main modes I play. After a long day at work, I like to come home and blast through Survival mode and just mow down zombies while building up my account level and gun level. I wish they would sell a Zombies standalone game. Multiplayer in BO7 is literally the worst of any CoD I have ever played. The vast majority of matches are extremely lopsided. Your first match of the day is almost guaranteed to put you in a match already in progress. This is because most of the team you just got dropped into has left the match. I have NEVER played a CoD where people leave so frequently, but I completely understand - it is not fun at all to get into a match where you are suddenly down by 150 points and you repeatedly get spawn killed. Multiple matches per night, I will get stuck on a team where more than half will just leave. I wish there was some balancing of ANY kind in these matches. I won't even get into the extremely toxic playerbase, since this is present in every online shooter game. Just turn off your text and voice chat. Campaign was fine - I didn't hate it as much as other posts. I wouldn't mind if CoD just dropped the Campaign modes altogether. Campaigns have never been something I was interested in, and I don't know a single other person who plays this mode. Store - everything is way overpriced, which is why you mainly just see Battle Pass skins in Multiplayer. There are some decent character skins in the Store, but they bundle it with a gun blueprint, a calling card, an emblem, an emote, etc., and they charge 1800-3000 CoD points for the bundle. People mainly just want the character skins - try breaking those out into separate items and see how many people buy. This is not new to BO7, and has been a CoD problem for years. If you want to check out BO7, wait for it to go on sale for $20 and just play Zombies.
categories: New Mechanic Reception, Monetization & Value, Story / Campaign, Community & Social

review: The current state of Black Ops 7 is disappointing; it feels as though the developers are over-catering to casual players, which inadvertently allows sweaty players to dominate by spamming AI-controlled scorestreaks in every match. A critical design flaw is the reliance on scorestreaks rather than traditional killstreaks; the former rewards volume over precision and disrupts the competitive balance. My advice to the developers is to prioritize game stability and core mechanics over cosmetic microtransactions. To create a truly high-quality experience, the movement systems need to be tuned down, as the current cracked movement speed makes engagements feel chaotic rather than skill-based. Until a significant balancing update is released, I cannot recommend this game as a worthwhile investment.

review: The worst COD to date easily. . . Multiplayer Review Only: -Unbalanced weapons in Core. Snipers can be ADS or Scoped faster than SMG's at times. Burst Assault Rifles are now in the Sniper/Marksman Categories with increased Burst Speeds and DMG, with no contenders. -Lobbies are seemingly unbalanced, if you've played XYZ amount of matches in ranked, then your only lobbies should be other ranked players. The general public doesn't need the sweatiest people in games every game. -Increased amount of cheaters going undetected. Reports need to capture the reported players last like 30 seconds of gameplay and have AI or People review it. To many people using filters to bypass smokes, or have straight up walls and know were people are, less aimbots in general but increased walls for sure. -Reports Require multiple people to report, seems to also have no active reporting with AI or Coding verifying reports. The pro's to this is sometimes some people get chat banned in match with enough reports and other times if to few people using mics reports users can spam slurs, and other things are just in chat and voice. -Maps, no point in them making new maps. The community votes on old BO2 or BO1 maps over and over and over. . . Personally the voting system needs removed or split all remake maps into it's own playlist, I enjoy the new maps good or bad tired of seeing nuketown, firing range, ect. (They are good maps doesn't mean i want to see it 1000 times for the next 20 years.) -Loadouts can't save multiple variants of the same gun. You have to have xyz attachments on the gun and it's shared between all loadouts of that gun. Can't have loadout 1 and loadout 2 with different attachments. -Lobbies are persistent lobbies up to 3 times matches then it'll look for a new lobby. I would prefer an option requesting new lobby search popup at the bottom at the end of matches and choose to search for a new lobby. Sometimes it's fun playing with ppl and sometimes the lobbies are so freaking sweaty you look for a new lobby asap. -AFK's are all over SND, the timer to detect 0 movement from spawn round after round is wild. Literally just put a detection box around spawn, if you didn't leave it round after round then kick them on second round. . . shouldn't see 2-5 afk's a match ever. -Leaving mid match, leaves games feeling empty. 60-120 second delays when abandoning a match should be added to dissuaded players from constantly leaving. To many matches are 3v6 or less. There's tons more issues i'm probably missing but these to me are the most toxic to just enjoying a casual match. Game runs fine probably the most optimized version since BO4.

review: Lets start with the campaign: it is extremely bad. Do NOT buy this game for the campaign. The story is weak, poorly written, incomplete, and hard to follow. The gameplay is boring, you cant pause, its always online, and you can even get kicked for inactivity in a solo campaign. The whole design just feels weird and unfinished. Theres also an endgame mode, a solo/co-op PvE experience on a large map with bots. Its kind of similar to MWZ, but in my opinion better. Its decent overall. Its great for leveling weapons, and you can level skills like in an RPG. It can be challenging and exciting at times. Its a nice change of pace and fun occasionally. Then theres Zombies. If you tweak some settings like the HUD, its actually pretty good. Theres the standard BO7 zombies mode and also a classic-style mode called Cursed Mode, which feels more like old-school zombies. There are multiple modes for different players and around 45 maps. The maps are good, gameplay is challenging and replayable. After 12 hours, zombies can become really funespecially Cursed Mode, which is very classic and well done. Now the multiplayer: it is EXTREMELY good. We havent had a multiplayer this good in a long time. No SBMM, fast lobbies, well-balanced matches. Leveling weapons feels satisfying, the camos are great, and the gunplayespecially the movementfeels amazing. Yes, the game is faster now. But thats what CoD is today. We just have to accept that CoD has become a movement shooter. Its not supposed to be realisticits supposed to be fast and arcade-like, and thats exactly what it is. And honestly, it works. Movement is smooth and fun: you can prone, slide, crouch, tac sprint, wall jump, climb quicklyeverything feels fast and fluid. You can play however you want. There are nearly 40 maps, including good new ones and great remakes. People hate BO7 way too much. Yes, the campaign is terrible. But Warzone is finally good again, multiplayer is basically perfect, zombies are solid, and theres even a completely new mode nobody asked forjust added for fun. Also, you can play campaign, endgame mode, and zombies in third-person, which is actually really cool. Final verdict: Multiplayer: Perfect Zombies: Good Endgame Mode: Okay Campaign: Absolute garbage If youre buying this for multiplayer, its 100% worth it. For zombies or endgame, its worth it toobut maybe wait for a sale. If youre buying it for the campaign just dont.

review: Black Ops 7's co op campaign is utterly overhated. Ironically, even though the format of its gameplay and presentation is very low brow, it is actually carried very well by some high brow topics that you almost never see in these campaigns, let alone Call of Duty. The campaign of Black Ops 7 is fundamentally more than just cooking off the fat that was left behind by Black Ops 6 admittingly far superior campaign, but within the same pan and oil that Black Ops 2 was made in. BLOPS7 is a surrealist, sci-fi cyberpunk hallucination-horror not unlike many of the best parts of Dead Space and the Batman Arkham games, as the Cradle gas is very literally the Scarecrow fear-toxin from DC. And once again, much like the previous mentioned games, a central component of these hallucinations besides AAA scary monsters and obviously gamified bossfights straight out of Doom or Wolfenstein, is actually trauma. For the first time probably ever besides Black Ops 2 hinting at it more than a few times with Woods (and it very lamely being somewhat alluded to in subsequent games like Cold War by Adler and Simms), Call of Duty is tackling survivor guilt, PTSD and the necessity of reaching out, having allies who have your back. What the ? I mean, it is pretty cool and enduring, but its completely surprising. Usually DoD adjacent games lean heavily into just the pseudo-supernatural when it comes to this stuff or uses it for movie-fied special scenarios. While it is true that is what the campaign mostly does, below the surface, there is an overlying theme that carries over far stronger than the absurdity of 6's campaign. I think a lot of this is just from the fact that Black Ops 2, whose writer was so well read and researched on the topic of rare resource driven, multipolar modern conflicts and the socioeconomics, industrial complexes behind them that he is now a recognized expert, is so well written that anything even vaguely affiliated with its story directly benefits from it. Black Ops is a franchise within a franchise that, at its best, completely overcomes both the Modern Warfare trilogies and at its worst, releases a multiplayer exclusive flop. And I have to say, this is the only multiplayer exclusive Call of Duty I can actually say has some substance beyond Nazi Zombies. Of course, a story driven so much by David Mason's own tragic relationship with his late dad, all while being saved by the good memories of it too, and other character's surprisingly realistic struggles in relation to events from prior games, is bound to be good by accident. Kagan also has a really great design and her performance is superb. The story would benefit from being single player and having far better pacing, that is true. Also, I opted to play it by myself which proved to be quite the challenge but also the most intense and enjoyable experience I've had with a COD campaign gameplay wise in some time. There are definitely many flaws that are validated further by the negative perception of the game overall, but is it all bad? Honestly, its actually pretty worth the money for a single playthrough or two of the co-op campaign and the bulk of the multiplayer, Endgame and zombies if you are already a loyalist to this series. For a casual? I honestly don't think it is nearly as accessible as some people said it is. I usually play COD just for the stories, which I know is insane to some nowadays. But I begrudgingly play to rank 55 usually and stop. This and 6 are ones where I have not done that, the moment the MP became unfun and punishing with SBMM and team balancing. I got pretty close, with rank 51, although half of that was earned via the co-op campaign and ultimately... I simply decided that once the game is not fun, there is no point in me playing it. But for the veteraned COD addicts, this is by far one of the best multiplayer outings in the modern series. I am getting older, I simply do not have the time, focus or ambition, nor friend group, to play these games with the same dedication I did with Black Ops 1 in the days of yore or even back in MW2022. Sadly, most of my friends have moved on from the COD franchise and its largely for the better, because its easy to lose respect for people who play a game and consistently piss themselves in anger over it. I've seen the absolute worst of some of my friends brought out of them by this game, even when they are winning consistently, the minute they don't 100-0 solo the lobby, they rage quit, uninstall and call it all sort of names, then reinstall it and beg to start a party again the next day. This toxic loop ended and since MW2023, I have been playing solo. Like I said before, I have way too many better things to be doing than just playing COD, but I would be lying if I said that I didn't enjoy my rare games where I absolutely devastated the lobby and constantly was the top player consecutively. The downside, even without SBMM in this entry, the team balance is still horrible. And your performance is truly tied to the competency of your team. The better you do consecutively, the worse your team will be the next game. And this is extremely persistent and impossible to ignore. I play Hardcore Hardline and Kill Confirmed almost exclusively when I am not doing Hardcore Nuketown 24/7, and I persistently am fighting dudes who are Prestige Master 1000 in a team of 500+s and my team is all rank 1-12s. I once had a game at rank 36 where my entire team was coincidentally 6 or 7 and the entire enemy team was 800+ with two 1000 PMs. The games prior I went 30+ and died maybe twice, the following games I went 3 and 17 while the enemy team had players rocking 20 kills and single digit deaths for everyone. Call of Duty really is not for the casuals anymore and it never was, truthfully. But I am officially at a skill ceiling of caring where I am just going to play for as long as I want and then stop forever. That being said, I probably will revisit the co-op campaign sometime in the future. Is it the best COD game? Uhm, in terms of gameplay? Yes, actually. By far. Other than that, it certainly will be quickly left behind for MW4. But this is the natural order of things.
categories: Usability Issues, New Mechanic Reception, Competitive Analysis, Monetization & Value, Story / Campaign, SBMM / Matchmaking Frustration, Community & Social

review: If you just need a Call of Duty game that is better than Black Ops 6 and feels great to play, buy this. The multiplayer and Zombies modes are a massive step up with tons of content. Just don't expect any groundbreaking innovation, and skip the campaign entirely. What I Loved The Movement & Gunplay: The movement system is top-tierit reminds me of Dirty Bomb, but even better. The gunplay is also fantastic and easily just as good as Modern Warfare 3. Everything just feels really nice. The Multiplayer Maps: There are a lot of maps here. I actually really like most of them, though a few are just ok-ish. Overall, it is way better than the Black Ops 6 multiplayer. Zombies Mode: It is just about as good as Black Ops 6 Zombies although not a lot of content yet. The Classic MW3 Vibes: This game really reminds me of the original Modern Warfare 3 days. Back then, the campaign got a lot of hate, but the multiplayer was a massive improvement over MW2. That is exactly how this game feels compared to BO6. What Needs Work (The Bad Stuff) Zero Innovation: Aside from the movement system, there is no real innovation here. The franchise has a ton of potential, but they just did the bare minimum. At its core, it is still just a run-and-gun shooter with toxic lobbies. Missed Opportunities: I would have loved to see some actual new game modes to push the series forward. For example: A mode where you push an objective, maybe mixed with the new Overload mode (which would require maps built specifically for it) think of Dirty Bomb for example. A mode with heavy environment destruction to compete with Battlefield which would also require new maps. A tactical mode to compete with Rainbow Six Siege which would also require new maps for it. All this sounds expensive and time consuming to make but I think it would be worth it for them if they just spent more time like maybe 2-3 years between games. Cheap AI Art: The obvious use of AI art in the game is super disappointing. It makes the whole experience feel cheap and lower quality. Terrible Campaign: I haven't even played the campaign because I already know it's bad. From what I've seen, it looks like an AI wrote the script. I might only play it eventually just to grind out the achievements, but it is not worth your time. Final Verdict It is not a perfect game. It completely lacks innovation and plays it way too safe. However, the core gameplay loop, movement, and amount of multiplayer/Zombies content make it a massive step up from Black Ops 6. Yes I know it's ironic I complained about AI art and wrote this with AI but this is based off my ideas and opinions that I wrote and then I refined the review myself. So yeah Gemini 3 Pro helped me write the review.

review: The game is actually not as bad as most reviews say, as the many negative ones were at the start. Note that I only play Zombies, due to it being the most fun I have in CoD in general! The performance is okay might I say. Of course it's not perfectly optimized, one cannot expect that anymore sadly, but as long as you dont use RT you should be fine. And if you have an AMD Card like me, then just go crazy, because this game is insanely good for AMD Cards! The easter eggs are cool (at the time of writing, there are only 4 maps), and reasonably complex too. It's fun, has a few cool new things, just good overall. Nothing to really mark as insanely good or insanely bad, as zombies is probably always good (in most cases at least). Do I recommend? Yes, especially if you like zombies, want higher graphical quality and can afford to play with higher settings. I do not recommend though, if you only want to play the campaign. You need to install the multiplayer DLC for it, because its designed for coop, with a sp option. Campaign itself is just weird. Do not buy it on normal price though! I got it for 44 on a sale recently, but the price normally is just absurd.

People have moaned about the story but in my opinion I enjoyed it, you do have to run it coop but I managed to play 3/4 of the game solo due to the negative hype around the game. The multiplayer is the same as always. It does feel like there is less game modes in multiplayer then the previous games. Again new zombies maps which are enjoyable. Endgame content is cool. I only pick up COD games every couple of years when I'm in the mood and it's scratched my itch. The content you actually get for the price makes up for the short campaign. review: Well done Treyarch! Definitely one of the best Call of Duty games ever made. It really feels like theyve finally figured out what direction the game should go. Treyarch has honestly done an incredible job so far. Weve gotten a new map for both Resurgence and Battle Royale, a brand new mode called Endgame, and they even brought back a Blackout-style Battle Royale experience without loadouts for the people who prefer a more classic BR feel. That alone deserves credit. On top of that, theyve updated both Rebirth Island and Verdansk, and now added new mechanics like wall jumping and the grapple hook, which make the game insanely fun to play. These mechanics completely change the pacing and overall flow of the game in a very good way. Ill be honest I was skeptical about those mechanics at first. I thought they might ruin the feel of Warzone. But after actually playing with them, I genuinely love them. They make the game feel fresh again. At this point, people who are still constantly trashing the game after everything Treyarch has done are honestly just hating for no reason. This is the best job theyve done in years, maybe ever. It really feels like Treyarch is finally listening to the community, and that deserves real praise. Massive credit to the devs. Theyve absolutely earned it.

review: This is the best Call of Duty in almost a decade. The movement feels amazing with wall jumping and omni-movement being fun additions, although, if you want simpler movement there is also a playlist with more classic movement. The Zombies is enjoyable but has a few bad parts. The matchmaking is also great compared to other recent Cod's and it doesn't try to optimize your engagement or playtime or whatever, and just lets you have fun. It also removed the terrible 'feature' of lobbies disbanding after every match that was added in a recent CoD. The only truly bad parts of this game are the ai calling cards and the campaign which is utterly terrible and the worst campaign ever. It is impossible for the campaign to have happened how it did with Karma being alive despite Harper also being alive and it just shows a lack of respect for Black Ops 2. Activision needs to let Treyarch develop their own in-house campaigns and I hope the backlash makes them realize that. If you liked Bo2 then this is probably the closest you'll get to that though.

review: I didn't buy this at launch and I'm glad I didn't. But now? It's actually in a fairly decent state. (TL;DR at the bottom) I'm primarily a Zombies player and its some of the most fun I've had in a while. Whether you're a classic purist or you embrace the new modern feel, there's modes for you. You play cursed or standard with both delivering a fun zombies experience. The maps are creative and fun, there's so much to dive into with 4 seasons of content added, I feel like I'm just at the tip of the iceberg. I haven't played the multiplayer all that much but after a few matches in the Black Ops Classic Mode, it's actually pretty nice. No sliding, no stims, it's call of duty stripped down to how it used to be. Even on only black ops 1 & 2 maps. Not sure why they didn't implement this originally but better late than never, it's actually really fresh and enjoyable. As for the endgame mode, it can be fun, it's basically just the DMZ mode from MWII but with no PvP. It's also F2P so you can try it without dropping any money at all. It's not awful, but it doesn't really stand out too much. I don't bother touching the campaign so my review isn't based on that either. This game is by no means perfect, and it doesn't really come anywhere close. But for what it is, it's actually not bad. I know it had an incredibly rocky start, maybe the worst ever, but Treyarch has done a lot to right their wrongs. The AI calling cards are still cringe and I hope they never do that again, but otherwise there's a lot to be enjoyed here. Your best bet is to wait until a sale, which shouldn't take long. With all the negative reviews this game has gotten it gets discounted pretty frequently. TL;DR: If you're looking for a fun game to turn your brain off to, this is more than likely the one - given that you know more or less what you're getting into. Zombies is by far the best selling point for me, and worth it just for that alone. The new Classic MP playlist is definitely worth checking out for long time cod fans. The Endgame mode is somewhat fun and has a nice gameplay loop, but you can check that out for free on the Blops7 store page. Haven't touched the campaign and likely never will. If any of that sounds good to you, get it on sale.

review: [h1]Haters gonna hate.[/h1] Dont be a f*ckin r*tard. Dont buy the f*ckin Vault Edition on release day for f*ckin 110 Euros and then cry about how bad the game is and how much it sucks and how sh*tty Call of Duty became in general and how greedy Activision is, simple as that. Youd be just another one of those idiots who still havent realized that the Call of Duty glory days of the original Modern Warfare Trilogy and the very first Black Ops game have been gone for far longer than a decade now and they will never come back. Get over it and either stop buying Call of Duty games all together or stop stumbling over the same stone every year again and then b*tch about how your own stupidity is other peoples fault. Now, with that out of the way, on to my short experience report with the Black Ops 7 campaign. Please take note that i already got to the point where i dont expect anything outstanding from a new Call of Duty game anymore a damn long time ago. For me, a new Call of Duty game is a mild success as soon as it has a solid campaign that is not as embarrassingly annoying and mediocre as Black Ops 3 (still the lowpoint to this day), so i usually just wait for at least half a year and a serious discount of at least 40 to 50%, before i even think about spending money on the standard edition. I did so once again with Black Ops 7 and what should I say? To be honest, Battlefield 6's campaign looks better, sounds better and just is a lot better, so if you are looking for the more rewarding and polished singleplayer experience, you should definitely go with BF6 this year. Nevertheless, at a 40% discount, I actually enjoyed Black Ops 7 quite a lot for a few hours as well. Sure, the so called story is so goofy, it goes even way beyond Black Ops 3 levels of stupidity, the leveldesign has more than a few rough edges and is pretty damn lazy at some points and i still wouldnt touch all that multiplayer warzone cesspool full of pubertal imbeciles even if somebody would put a gun to my head. On top, the grafics really start to show their age and in general the CoD games dont seem to have quite the production values they still had half a decade ago anymore. But damn, if i would say that i didnt enjoy this at least a lot more than Black Ops 6s campaign again, then id be a f*ckin liar. Once more, this is all i can say: The OG Modern Warfare from 2007 was CoDs absolute peak (both in terms of single- and multiplayer!), and if you are able to finally abandon your futile hopes, properly manage your expectations and conceive that this franchise will never ever even come close to such a masterpiece again, then chances are that you can enjoy some Black Ops 7 for what its worth, too. review: I've played every CoD throughout the years, experiencing the series gradual shift from Arcade shooter to Movement shooter. Blops7 is by far the nth degree of the cracked out mentality. If you're not wall jumping, sliding, diving, and playing piano with your controls... you're probably not gonna have a good time. I'm good at twitch shooters, but performing the same way every match gets very stale very fast. Rather than mixing up the formula with creative loadouts that would support different playstyles, we get a franchise that's doubled down on its most extreme meta. It doesn't matter what you spawn with, or what the objective is. There is only one way to play this game.

review: Finally decided to get BO7 because I have grown bored of everything else. I avoided this at first because everyone was saying it was bad, so I thought they were right. Boy was I wrong. This game has been so fun. The movement is awesome. Its not insane like advanced warfare, but the wall jump is just enough extra to be cool but not too much. Other than that its basically normal Call of Duty but with omnidirectional movement which is minor honestly but pretty sweet. The game runs and plays perfectly. I have become instantly addicted to team deathmatch again. The gunplay feels perfect and its so refreshing to not have to empty 3 clips into ememies for them to die, and to have everyone on an even playing field for HP. I have missed COD and I didnt even know it. This game is really fun. I havent even dipped into zombies yet which I heard they brought back round based, which I am super pumped to play. Happy with my purchase. **For the concerned people, Every review says 0 hours on record because it registers like a DLC, but playtime is counted on the main game launcher** Yes this is a real review by a real dude. review: Look, Im just going to be real: I love this game. Ive loved every CoD thats dropped over the last few years, and Black Ops 7 is no different. Is it frustrating that every single November I have to kiss my camos goodbye and start from level zero. Its a massive pain. But at the end of the day, its just a game. If youre stressing that hard over a digital thing, youre missing the point. The Nexus Dream Im still waiting for the day Activision realizes we just want a Universal CoD. Give us the CoD Mobile treatment on PC/Console. Give us one launcher where the seasons just keep rolling, the content stays fresh, and the file size doesn't take up 500GB of my SSD. Just one permanent home for the grind. The Love-Hate Cycle I play this game 4-5 hours a day. Do I want to give myself the bullet after a session of bo7 is throwing me into a sweat-fest? All the time. But thats just the bitter taste of CoD. You either accept the toxicity and the sweat, or you get off the court. We all know these games are designed to squeeze every cent out of our pockets. Its 2026thats just the industry now. Its sad, but it is what it is. You have to focus on the fun side and stop letting a video game dictate your mental health. If you're getting genuinely angry, take a walk. The Hate Trend The funniest part is the community. People hate on the new CoD because its a trend to hate it. Year 1: This game is trash, Im never playing again. Year 5: Wait, remember Black Ops 7? That was a masterpiece. Its the CoD Ghosts effect. Everyone dragged that game through the mud when it dropped, and now my YouTube feed is nothing but: Why CoD Ghosts was actually a Top 3 CoD. Everyone misses the old game as soon as the new one arrives. Bottom line: Im having a blast. Stop following the hate train and just play. (Anyway, Im mostly writing this long-winded review so I can level up my Steam profile.)
categories: New Mechanic Reception, Community & Social

review: This is a Review for the Zombies Mode, as that is where I spent the entirety of my time. I think the Zombies Mode is Top Notch, and is the best of the Modern Zombies formula. The Maps Release up until this point have been great. Ashes is the Fan Favorite, Astra is my personal favorite and Paradox has pleasantly surprised me the more Ive played it. There are still 3 maps to be released , so my opinion as a whole may change over time but the current offering is great. The Easter Eggs are fun to complete, Cursed mode gives more replay-ability and classic zombies style scoring. And survival is great for casual rounds to grind xp and weapons skins. Im personally not a fan of the Gobblegum system so I dont really like it being back and so heavily monetized. But the Augment System brought back from BO6 is a welcomed and fun addition. All and all if youre a fan of CoD Zombies my recommendation is NOT to skip this title. Its definitely worth experiencing. Wait for a sale, get it for a good price and enjoy

review: This is a mixed review not everyone will agree with me, and thats fine. Gameplay: The core idea is actually good, but the overall vibe feels unfinished. Its like there was potential here, but it never fully came together. Graphics: Visually, the game looks solid at first glance, but once you pay attention, some details feel off or inconsistent. Voice Acting: This is one of the biggest letdowns. The characters feel flat and lack the emotion that previous games had. Its especially noticeable if youve played earlier Black Ops titles. Out-of-the-box experience: Honestly, this is frustrating. Too many ads when launching the game Being asked to restart for updates when everything is already up to date Even more ads when selecting Black Ops 7 Some ads cant be skipped It completely breaks immersion. On top of that, some operators feel out of place for Call of Duty like, why does it feel like Fallout 4 content is creeping in? Changes from Beta: I dont like what was done with Takeos voice it doesnt feel the same. A lot of changes from the beta also made things feel worse or harder to enjoy. Final Thoughts The game isnt terrible but it doesnt feel finished. It comes off like it needed more time and polish before release. Recommendation: Wait for a discount. At $70, it doesnt feel worth it based on the current quality. A more reasonable price would be around $20$40.

review: During the lead up to Black Ops 7's release, Call of Duty made a big deal about the discontinuation of carry forward, the cancellation of collaborative cosmetics, and removal of SBMM from casual playlists. Turns out that this was nothing but a smokescreen to get people's feet in the door of what may be the laziest video game ever produced. Black Ops 7's multiplayer barely innovates on its predecessor. While Omni-movement remains an under-utilized gimmick, the addition of wall jumping does add a useful tool for navigating combat. Unfortunately, the multiplayer maps are scared to properly utilize the mechanic to its fullest potential like we saw with wall-running in Black Ops 3, and some maps don't have any real use for it at all. Most egregious among these are the reprisals from Black Ops 2, which dominate the map vote but have not been adapted to the new gameplay mechanics, rendering them all but useless. Add to that the new maps being mediocre at best and abysmal at worst, creating another entry of mediocre multiplayer modes unwilling to innovate their design. I have not played the Zombies mode in my 9 hours of playtime, but considering that I have been playing Call of Duty since 2010, and enjoyed Zombies a whole lot over these past 15 years, the fact that the return of the original Zombies cast failed to drum up any real interest in playing the mode in the slightest does not fill me with confidence. Plus, they decided to make a spiritual successor to TranZit, and I do not recall that map being particularly well-loved. And, of course, the campaign. You have undoubtedly heard things about Black Ops 7's campaign. Whatever you think you know, I assure you it is 10 times worse. The plot is incoherent, and the game's villain is all but non-existent. Not for lack of a villain, mind you, as the game immediately reveals its big plot twist 5 minutes in, but because they play almost zero role in the plot and their evil plan is laughably predictable. The boss fights are all terrible, and all the missions have at least one. Half the time it is some sort of Guild hardware, either a turret or a VTOL or a robot, and the other half its some random Multiplayer operator that teleports whenever you shoot them. Both are terrible. Giant Harper is also terrible, but you've seen Giant Harper. Hilariously enough, the best boss fight is the one that I can only assume was made by someone who really likes fighting the Pyro Regisvine from Genshin Impact, but even that is horrendously bad. The game has 11 missions, with every other mission being walking around the presumably upcoming Warzone map for 20 minutes, a mode whose mechanics are also littered throughout the campaign. The other half the missions are all reused assets, mostly multiplayer maps, and including one egregious example where the game reuses a portion of one of Black Ops 6's campaign missions in Vorkuta. The main cast can barely be considered characters. David Mason continues his previous role of Standard White Man in a CoD game, Harper has been reduced to rage and one-liners, 50/50 has some vaguely tragic backstory about a massacre in Japan which she was present for for some reason, and Samuels is there. Ironically, the best mission in the game is the one where you play as JSOC's multiplayer operators, who actually have personalities. The Endgame mode is not worth playing the campaign to unlock, and this is coming from someone who played a whole lot of MWII's DMZ. Two final things: 1. This game has an odd obsession with Japan, to the point that it was seemingly the focal point of the game's marketing. This means half the multiplayer maps are inexplicably set in Japan. It's especially odd since they said that this would be the first Black Ops with maps set in Japan, despite BO2's Magma and BO4's Gridlock being set in Japan. 2. The very big issue: Activision has riddled this game with AI-generated assets. Calling Cards, Prestige icons, and I would not be shocked if it's eventual storefront used AI art as well. There is even speculation that AI was used to write the campaign, but these are unfounded and I'm not sure that I believe it, regardless of how bad it was. Either way, Activision has made it abundantly clear that this was a conscious choice and seemingly has no intentions of changing course. In a year in which DICE and EA did everything in its power to revitalize the public perception of the Battlefield franchise, Call of Duty has decided to cement itself as a pillar to corporate greed and a giant middle finger to the concept of video games as creative works. Activision has actively decided that the most profitable video game franchise in history would be reduced to nothing but slop in favor of making as much money as possible as it reuses assets, plot points, and characters from previous games and screw over its artists as it generates its most intricate 2D assets the series has had for over 15 years. My only hope is that someone at Activision realizes the blight they have created in this medium and the company does a massive course correction in the coming years, but I know that this is a monster created by myself and the millions of others that have bought increasingly lazy CoD games over the past few years. The one good thing to come out of this game is that it has finally succeeded in breaking me free of the Stockholm Syndrome this series has locked me in for the past decade. Small edit to clear up a thing that's come up: I have 9 hours in this game. The reason it says I have 0 hours is because Black Ops 7 boots up through the Call of Duty launcher, since buying this game does not give you the game, but rather access to its files on that application. Hence, all my playtime is on the Call of Duty app, since you can't have playtime on Black Ops 7 specifically since its not an application. Also, for the record, I tried refunding this game 4 times and it was rejected every time. And I expected the game to be the normal amount of bad, hence why I bought it. Accept your cautionary tale.

review: When I think about the worst single-player FPS campaigns Ive played in the last 25 years, titles like Far Cry New Dawn, Wolfenstein: Youngblood, Halo Infinite, Rage 2, Killzone Shadow Fall, or even Modern Warfare III come to mind. And yet, all of those are masterpieces compared to this soulless abomination disguised as a video gamea product forged in the fires of pure corporate greed. It has so many baffling design decisions: - A single-player campaign wrapped in a Warzone-flavored multiplayer shell - XP and unlocks in single-player - No pause button (because who needs basic human functions?) - Health bars on enemies (aka bullet-sponges) - Cutscenes. So many cutscenes. - Packet burst (in a single-player campaign) - Giant spiders. - Giant insects. - A tree made of Frank Woods. - Wandering through empty Warzone maps while being shot by stationary idiots - You cant pick up enemy weapons (a feature thats worked for two decadesgone!) - Completely chaotic tone: cyborgs, zombies, ninjas, soldiers all in the first 20 minutes - A boss fight in those same 20 minutesonly winnable with killstreaks - Trippy hallucination sequences (the part nobody likes, but they keep doing anyway) - Invisible teammates because its secretly a co-op campaign for some reason - UI straight out of a mobile game - And overall the vibe of no one talked to anyone and we shipped it anyway This has to be a joke, right? Coming off Arc Raiders, this feels like something dug up from 2014. Its painfully clear the devs were forced to crank this out under corporate pressure. Im not even a CoD haterI loved Infinite Warfare, liked Vanguard, and even thought the MW3 campaign was fine. But this? Yes, I paid money for it. Yes, I regret it. And yes, I absolutely shouldve known better. Anyway time to head back topside, Raiders.

review: The ONLY reason I have this is to play zombies with my brother, DO NOT BUY THIS GAME. It is rushed, AI ridden and blatantly a cash grab by Activision. I feel sorry for the individual devs who had to put this out knowing the reception it would be getting. Let this be a lesson, Activision, do not make your dev teams crunch out a game in a year right after making the last one and absolutely do not use AI generated art. Updating now that I have played the mode I got this game for and what a tragedy... this zombies is AMAZING it builds on BO6 zombies strengths and has massively better art direction and writing. No longer is zombies taking itself too seriously, it is back to being a bit silly with the occasional serious moment. The visuals are outstanding, much more dark and moody with plenty of callbacks to previous zombies maps in a interesting and genuinely inspired way. So why is this a tragedy? This amazing mode is forever linked to the main game experience, you have a prime steak next to microwave food that has been left out for a year, you have amazing art direction next to AI generated calling cards. And, as much as this zombies is amazing it is not 70-100 amazing on its own. I genuinely think it's time Activision should really consider a standalone zombies game. A game where all the focus is on the zombies experience and not having to ration resources between multiplayer, campaign or zombies. Give us a zombies game, the playerbase is there and being free of being a side experience could elevate the mode and give the zombies team real room to have fun. But who am I kidding? This is Activision, more AI generated art for you and please buy our 200 store packs while we work our teams to death by making them make games in a year instead of the 3 they are supposed to, Just look, Activision, people love zombies and it really could be a hit to have it stand on its own instead of being a niche side community of COD. [h1]TL:DR Main game is plagued with issues but Zombies is outstanding but it is painful that such a good zombies experience is tied to one of the worst COD launches of all time[/h1]

review: I'm not a COD hater but this has to be the worst game they've ever released. I'll breakdown each game mode and why it's so bad. Campaign - No real set pieces, terrible writing, confusing plot, multiple plot holes and zero replay value. The Bosses here are extremely spongey and offer very little satisfaction or challenge. This whole campaign feels rushed and unfinished. Not to mention a lot of the moments shown in the previews for this game don't appear in the final campaign. Most of the marketed moments don't actually happen in the campaign. Multiplayer - They gutted everything good about Black Ops 6 MP and it's movement. The game now feels extremely clunky with various design decisions fighting each other. They want you to bounce off the walls while also moving like a snail on the ground. Zombies - Removal of LTMs and modes like Grief. Ashes is definitely better than Terminus but Vandorn Farm doesn't fill the gap left by Liberty Falls. Like the rest of the game it feels shallow and doesn't entice me to play it more, if anything it's the opposite. Endgame - Reskinned MWZ with essentially no progression. They reskinned zombies perks into skill trees and killstreaks into minor abilities. The whole mode is essentially driven by camo grinding and unlocking operators. Pre-Order - Advertised skins and bonuses were cut after the game was already for sale. Essentially this means players pre-ordered the game under the guise that certain content would be made available to them which Treyarch then cancelled without proper notice. This goes beyond COD and could set a dangerous precedent for pre-order bonuses moving forward. Carry Forward - This was also advertised as a feature and was only changed after pre-orders went live. The biggest problem I have with this is that they cancelled it only to selectively carry forward specific pieces of content and ultimately costing the players 2/3 of the weapon arsenal. Instead of scrapping the skins and just bringing forward the base versions of the guns themselves they essentially just cut all that content and it shows. The game feels like an empty husk and I'm not sure new seasons will add enough content to offset that feeling.
categories: Usability Issues, New Mechanic Reception, Competitive Analysis, Monetization & Value, Story / Campaign, Community & Social

review: This is the best example of corporate greed, lies, and nostalgia bait that I have ever seen. Activision likes to redefine terms like minimal to desperately try to recover from Battlefield 6 and Arc Raiders. They have outright lied about SBMM being minimal in the open playlists. They use terms like minimal to prevent legal accountability from straight up lying to the fan base about removing SBMM from the default match making. Every lobby I have played (open playlist or standard) have been absolute 1 to 1 clones of myself in terms of skill level. This makes for the casual lobbies (non ranked) being the most competitive and sweaty, non-casual experience. Want to grind for camos, challenges, achievements, or calling cards? Good luck! If you use anything other than the meta weapons and try going for a challenge rather than playing at your 100% best, you're just going to get stomped on.

review: Its hard to believe this is the same franchise that once defined the genre. The campaign is absolute drivel; a disjointed mess of set pieces that lacks any of the soul or stakes found in the early Modern Warfare or Black Ops eras. It feels like it was written by an AI trying to mimic a blockbuster and failing. The multiplayer is even worse. Inconsistency is the only thing its consistent at; between the erratic hit registration and the frustrating 'engagement-based' matchmaking, you never feel like youre actually in control of your performance. This isn't the Call of Duty I grew up with. Its a hollowed-out corporate shell of its former self. An absolute embarrassment to the glory days.

review: Terrible campaign I refuse to view as a canon sequel to BO2, Great Multiplayer, Great Zombies. Welcome to Black Ops 3 2.0 As much as I love playing the Multiplayer and Zombies (seriously Treyarch. Good Job, and Welcome back Craig Houston to the zombies writing team) I cannot in GOOD CONSCIENCE make this a positive review due to Activision's corporate greed. Let's start off with the good, so people know i'm not mindlessly hating or saying some bs. Multiplayer: -The removal of mandatory SBMM in MP: Probably one of the best decisions you guys made in for so long. I was confident that if you removed it from multiplayer, it would be received so much better. Come Nov 14; I and many other people were proven right. This is one of the most fun experiences I've had in the multiplayer mode by far. -Mechanics: Personally, I love getting a bonus from mixing different colored perks, it allowed for more variety than just having the same three colored perks all match. The movement in this game is superb. It feels so much more satisfying to hit a wall jump kill with a shotgun, than just tac sprint into a slide cancel with a weapon that has a ridiculous ttk. Which goes onto my next segment. -Weapons: The guns in this game feel fine, nothing more i could say because it actually feels like i have a fighting chance compared to someone that has 10 meta attachments to their gun and laser beamed me before i even had a chance to react. (Note: this positive might be sensitive to change depending on the coming seasons) Zombies -The Crew: I won't lie, I was extremely worried about the OG 4 coming back being straight up nostalgia bait (as much as I love hearing Nolan, Steve, and Freddy again). But I'm pleasantly surprised. I could tell Steve was extremely enthusiastic about being Tank again. Because if you turn on the subtitles, you can notice Dempsey will say more than what subtitles have written down (as you can tell I zeroed in on Tank Dempsey because that is my goat). Could be an error, but it's funnier to think that way. They get along well with Requiem save for Richtofen, cus you know what timeline he's from. -Takeo: To Nelson Lee, the new voice actor for Takeo. Spectacular job. I cannot stress this enough, you are doing wonderful with your take on Takeo. You were given big shoes to fill with Tom Kane's legacy and you outdid my expectations. I look forward to hearing more in the coming maps. -Gameplay: The base gameplay is similar to Bo6, which is pretty solid albeit tac sprint is removed (thank god). I still appreciate how armor is justified with the zombies' damage scaling. However, there are mechanics and some major quality of life improvements i noticed, that make it very different from Bo6 Zombies. So genuinely, great job on taking criticisms from the beta -Story: The real meat, is the Story. As much as i disliked the latter parts of Bo6 zombies' story, this looks very promising. Though i will not be putting major spoilers, they call back to Cold War Zombies, which i love so much, Bo6 was supposed to do that in my eyes however. I love the side easter egg for Dempsey where you go through his PTSD. Which makes me wonder if they're going to do that with every character. Again Craig, welcome back. Please don't abandon us i beg of you. Dead Ops Arcade: If you have Dead Ops Arcade, i'm going to love it. Simple as. With the things i love about the game out of the way. Let's go into the reasons why I am leaving this a negative review. -The Use of AI: This is to Activision. I and a good 80% of the community don't really care if everyone's using AI like you had some poor intern write in your statement. That's not an excuse for laziness (which is using up everyone's water and electricity) and making the game look cheap just so you could pocket a couple of bucks that would barely make even a scratch in your wallet if you just payed some actual artists instead. I could tell you used GrokAI to generate a MASSIVE percentage of the calling cards with the soulless studio ghibli style and piss filter. It's just tone deaf to your audience, your dev team, and an insult to the beauty in art. That's one of the major reasons your competitors are outperforming you. Please the love of god, just stop using AI. -The Campaign: This story had to be generated by ChatGPT. I'm sorry if that insults anyone on the writing team, if it wasn't. But this story is just bad, idk what to say, I'm still processing how terrible it is. The gameplay is just Warzone: The Movie. With a weapon upgrade system (why?), Boss battles being big hallucinations (Oh my god bruh). It just felt like slog to play, i could not make it past the first mission and watched the entire story. You saw the positive reception for one of the most unique missions in BO6 and went: Hey! lets make this the entire campaign mission, thats a good idea! -LACK OF A PAUSE BUTTON!!!: Hey, I need to use the bathroom every now and then. Why does the campaign not have a pause button on Solo? Why does the Solo Zombies experience still have a fifteen minute timer for pausing when it used to be infinite in the past? This is still one of the most consumer hostile features in a game by far. What if I need to put my clothes away? What if I'm constipated and stuck in the bathroom? What I have chores to do? What if i have to eat something that isn't a snack? I can't do that with just fifteen minutes. Save and Quit's a great feature, but I would still love the ability to pause my game and take a long break before hopping back on without needing to load in again. It's just not enough time for something like zombies, and one of the worst decisions for making a campaign. Please just bring back offline servers, it will be received so much better that way. Overall this game without the AI is a 8/10. With the campaign making it suffer a little. But the Multiplayer and Zombies definitely make up for that. With the AI, it's brought down to a whopping 5/10 and i'm being really generous with that no insult to the devs, except for the AI team wholeheartedly. Learn how to draw or write something. There is my honest review for Call of Duty: Black Ops 7.

review: Title: Surprisingly solid don't believe all the hate Review: I almost didn't buy this because of all the negative buzz, but honestly? I'm glad I ignored it. Yeah, the AI calling cards are a thing. Yeah, it's annoying. But here's the thing they don't affect gameplay at all. Let's be real: you're not buying a Call of Duty game to stare at calling cards in the menus. What actually matters is what you're playing, and Black Ops 7 delivers where it counts. Multiplayer is genuinely great. The Omnimovement feels even smoother than last year, and wall-jumping adds a fun new layer of mobility without breaking the flow . The 20v20 Skirmish mode is a blast, it gives me those old-school Battlefield vibes with wingsuit respawns and actual team coordination . And persistent lobbies are back! I've had some awesome rivalries with the same group of players across multiple matches, something we lost in recent years . Zombies is solid. The new map is massive, Ol' Tessie is a fun addition, and round-based survival is back with all the classic chaos you expect . Plus, the save feature lets you pick up where you left off if you're playing solo huge quality-of-life win. About the SBMM controversy they actually toned it down in casual playlists. For once, I can jump into matches without sweating every round. It feels like old-school CoD where you could actually relax and have fun . The campaign is weird, I'll admit. Giant Michael Rooker and plant monsters? Sure, it's out there. But honestly, playing it co-op with friends turned it into a hilarious experience . Sometimes dumb fun is still fun. Look, if you're buying CoD for calling card art, maybe skip it. But if you care about gameplay , tight gunplay, smooth movement, strong Zombies, and genuinely fun multiplayer, this is worth picking up. Ignore the outrage, give it a shot. Hours played: after this review written = 1.8k hours+ and still counting Level : Prestige Master 365

And I understand it beceause a bad game is very suffocating you literally can't move after spawning. Some maps just doesn't fit the art direction of the game, they feel like it's map that have been cut from BO6. Campaign : Worst COD campaign ever, they ruined all the characters I used to love in the black ops 1 and 2. It just doesn't feel like a campaign, it's like 4 hours of DMZ mission. The worst part is that even campaign now works only with servers... I'm lagging in my solo campaign that is just not normal. Zombie : Very good, I still doesn't like the warzone direction that they took with cold war but the zombie here is pretty nice. The cursed mod is great, the first map and the survival are cool. This is the real gem in this game. Dead Ops Arcade 4 is really fun too ! Do not buy it, wait for a discount for the zombie. The sales are bad so they should be cutting a lot of content to focus on MW4... which is a shame considering how good the zombie is in this game.
